value.market_read: Headline: Cushing fell to 23.0 mb, down 2.79 mb on the week and now already below the ~25 mb operational floor — the second straight week sub-floor after May 15's 25.82 mb print. The hub has bled 6.75 mb in four weeks (29.77 → 23.02 mb), a relentless trajectory that hands physical deliverability into a fragile state. Total US crude drew 3.33 mb to 441.7 mb as refiners ramped hard. Driver: Refinery utilization jumped 2.9 pts to 94.5%, with crude inputs at 16.97 mb/d (+0.65 mb/d w/w) pulling barrels into the system. PADD II util surged 8.8 pts to 95.9% as Midwest turnarounds wrapped, directly draining the Cushing tank farm. The 4-week crude import average eased to 5.65 mb/d from 5.86 mb/d, removing a supply cushion just as runs peaked. Trade flows: Crude exports softened to 5.07 mb/d (4-wk) from 5.15 mb/d, keeping incremental barrels stateside but not enough to offset the run-rate pull at Cushing. Gasoline drew 2.57 mb to 211.6 mb and distillate fell 2.11 mb to 100.8 mb, both consistent with firming late-May driving-season demand — gasoline 4-wk demand steady at 8.9 mb/d. Watch next week: Cushing deliverability is the dominant story. With tanks sub-floor and runs near 95%, watch WTI prompt backwardation — any steepening signals scramble for prompt barrels. A third consecutive sub-floor draw would intensify physical stress into the heart of hurricane season.

padd.padd1: Gasoline drew 1.84 mb as PADD I refinery utilization slid 3.9 pts to 85.4%, trimming local supply into firming Memorial Day demand. Distillate eased 0.31 mb and jet ticked down 0.09 mb, while crude built a modest 0.92 mb. With NYH RBOB structure leaning backwardated into the holiday, prompt premiums should pull barrels north via Colonial — accelerating any subsequent build. Watch: Colonial nominations and NY Harbor RBOB cash diffs — sustained backwardation means more waterborne and pipeline barrels arriving into PADD I next week.
padd.padd2: Cushing crashed 2.79 mb to 23.0 mb, already below the ~25 mb operational floor and now two weeks running sub-floor. PADD II utilization leapt 8.8 pts to 95.9% as turnarounds completed, with the regional crude draw of 4.71 mb the engine behind the tank-farm bleed. Gasoline drew 1.32 mb and distillate eased 0.13 mb on healthy runs. The four-week 6.75 mb drawdown leaves deliverability genuinely stressed. Watch: WTI prompt backwardation and any third sub-floor weekly draw — tank minimums are now the binding constraint on physical delivery.
padd.padd3: Gulf Coast utilization hit 98.0%, up 0.8 pts to a near-peak run rate that pushed gasoline up 1.52 mb and jet up 1.05 mb. Distillate drew 1.26 mb, the standout product move, likely on export pull as PADD III barrels clear to waterborne markets. Crude was essentially flat (-0.01 mb) with regional runs maxed. With hurricane season open since June 1, any storm risk against 98% utilization is asymmetric. Watch: Atlantic disturbance formation and LOOP sour differentials — a single landfall threat against peak runs would whipsaw both crack spreads and export schedules.
padd.padd4: Rocky Mountain utilization vaulted 12.6 pts to 90.9% as turnaround season unwound, lifting runs sharply off recent lows. Crude drew 0.42 mb and distillate fell 0.4 mb as ramped throughput consumed regional barrels. Gasoline was flat (+0.04 mb) and jet barely moved. The util swing is the dominant signal in this small but turnaround-sensitive market. Watch: Bakken and DJ Basin crude availability — with runs back near 91%, any feedstock tightness could squeeze the thin regional balance quickly.
padd.padd5: West Coast gasoline drew 0.97 mb with CARB-spec isolation keeping the LA Basin balance tight. PADD V utilization edged up 0.7 pts to 82.3%, the laggard among regions, while crude built 0.9 mb. Distillate and jet were near-flat (-0.02 and -0.2 mb). The gasoline draw against soft runs suggests demand is absorbing supply faster than refiners are adding it. Watch: LA Basin margins and Phillips 66 Rodeo conversion impacts — sub-83% utilization leaves the region thin if any unplanned outage hits CARB supply.
